summary: High‑quality soundboard capture of a late‑1971 show featuring Keith Godchaux’s debut on organ and several standout Jerry Garcia moments.
review: The remastered soundboard source is clear and well balanced overall, though a few listeners note a slight loss of clarity compared with other mixes and some wow‑and‑flutter on Jack Straw. Musically the concert shines with Keith’s first organ work on Dark Star, a soaring Jerry solo on Me & Bobby McGee, and energetic renditions of Tennessee Jed and Johnny B. Goode, while a clunky Truckin' start and a trimmed Casey Jones intro keep it from being flawless.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews are 4‑5 stars praising the performance and the remastered soundboard, with only minor criticisms; ample detailed feedback provides strong confidence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Nice remaster by Jay; generally clear and balanced but a bit less crisp than other Bertha/Ashley mixes, some wow‑and‑flutter on Jack Straw and slight balance issues.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Two‑set show with a short (~1 hr) second set; house lights stayed on all night, adding an odd atmosphere. Overall pacing is energetic with strong early‑night jams and a few rough edges.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Frequent praise for his biting Strat tone, soaring solos (Me & Bobby McGee), inventive fills and overall tight playing.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned sweeping bass underneath the Big Railroad duel and generally solid foundation.
band_member_comments.Bob: Highlighted in vocal duels on Big Railroad and noted as a "new man on keys" reacting to Keith’s organ work.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith Godchaux praised for his Hammond B‑3 organ debut, barrel‑housing style and rapid improvement over the next months.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann referenced as part of the rhythm section; no specific critique beyond standard support.
